developer_response.body:
Hi AnnarchieMorrigan,

Thank you for sharing your honest feedback. We understand that some recent updates and events may not have matched the experience you were hoping for, and we’re sorry for the frustration that caused.

We would also like to clarify that there are currently far more rods and reels available through in-game Credits and Golden Tokens than through In-App Purchases. Most progression gear can be earned simply by playing the game.

In-App Purchase items are mainly designed around cosmetic themes, convenience, or personal preference, and they are not intended to provide stronger gameplay advantages. The stats are balanced to avoid creating a pay-to-win experience.

Regarding the Shark Frenzy event, we know some players have felt the Megalodon appearance rate was too inconsistent or time-consuming. We’ve been actively reviewing feedback internally throughout the event, and we are already discussing ways to improve the balance between challenge and relaxation for future shark events.

We also want to clarify that we do not believe player feedback is “wrong” or unimportant. Even when opinions differ internally or within the community, we read the feedback carefully and continue discussing it as a team. Critical feedback like yours is part of that process as well.

Thank you again for taking the time to share your thoughts.

reviews.review_description:
I'll get this out of the way first, from a gameplay and game design standpoint, it's arguably the best fishing game on the Quest. Gameplay is rock solid. I'm not going to go into depth on that stuff,  I believe there a million reviews out there.

BUT...I thought I was the only one to be getting absolutely fed up with the micro transactions littering my screen, but I see many others are fed up with it too.

I do understand that these locations do need to be photographed  with a 360 camera and mapped accurately to the topology, and undoubtedly requires actually traveling to all the locations.  I get that all costs money and it's beyond a typical polygonal character being designed in a studio.

So I do get why not all of these updates can be released for free, especially on the VR platform which is more niche than mainstream flat gaming. But for the LOVE OF GOD, get these DLC screens out of my face.

Clicking on the map brings up all these pins that look like you can play, and you're spending time clicking on them only to reveal 5 DLCs on each screen. So now you're putting it on me to memorize every location I have access to play and take it upon myself to avoid the DLC landmines?

Secondly, there is no preview at all of what you're even getting with a DLC. All you see in the game is a pin on the map, click it and you get a DLC box. No idea how many new maps come with it, what they are, what they look like. 

At least hide these from the main map and add a second screen that shows all of the DLCs available for purchase. Once purchased, it goes to your main map.

The base game is $20, and after that you can still spend nearly $100 on these maps. It's beyond invasive. 

UPDATE: I upped the score to 3 stars. I was a bit hard on it. The game itself is solid, beautiful, well designed and FUN. Hopefully in the future we can have a bit less invasive DLC screens.

developer_response.body:
Hi ChickenMuphy,

Thanks for taking the time to share your thoughts with us and provide us with feedback.

Regarding the DLC structure, as you know, operating the game and creating new content incurs costs, so we released the DLCs with new maps and fish at a reasonable price. Also, as you may have noticed, we release new free events in our game every two weeks to provide our users with a new experience. We'll discuss internally how we can provide new experiences to our users in the most cost-effective way possible and review our update policy.

Regarding your opinion on the DLC preview, it's something we need to consider. We will discuss it and consider making it available.

Regarding the AI flag, I used AI to organize the response, which caused the flag. We apologize for the inconvenience, but we review all feedback and try to incorporate it into our game. 

Thanks again for taking the time to write a review and share your thoughts. We'll do our best to provide you with the best experience possible.

HiAFCB_on_top,

We understand that some long-time players prefer the simpler and more realistic style that Real VR Fishing focused on in earlier years. As the game has grown, we've experimented with a wider variety of content, including seasonal events, special challenges, and themed experiences, in an effort to provide new things for players to enjoy.

reviews.review_title: WAS Absolutely the best Fishing game
reviews.review_description:
This used to be a great game. Now the devs are turning it into a pay-to-play cash grab.
They’re sucking the fun right out of what this game used to be. Greed ruins everything eventually, and it looks like this game finally caught the disease. What was once a relaxing game is now stuffed full of monetization systems begging for your real money. Dumb outfits, overpriced rods, constant pushes to spend.
I never had a problem buying DLCs to support the game. That’s normal. But what they’re doing now isn’t support… they’re elbow-deep in your wallet.
And don’t just take my word for it. Go read the real reviews, not the suspicious flood of shiny fake 5-star ones.
The admins and devs are blowing smoke up your tail pipe trying to convince everyone this is somehow good for the game while quietly building a bigger paywall every update.
Then you’ve got people like “Susy” running around ruining rooms for everyone else while nothing gets done about it.
I already posted a list of over 20 game developers that went belly-up in the last few years after switching to aggressive pay-to-play models. This game looks like it’s heading down the exact same road.
At this point it feels like they’re just squeezing the community for quick cash so they can fund the next game they’re pushing… which will probably have the same paywall garbage baked into it.
Same story. Different game.

reviews.review_title: It's fun until they slap you with micros
reviews.review_description:
It’s a good fishing game, and it was nice to get to fish again since I can’t IRL thanks to being put in a wheelchair, but they give you this whole spiel about going through the journey and becoming the greatest fisherman, blah blah blah, and then only give you the first five parts of the journey, and you can’t progress without buying DLC.

I got mine through Horizon+, but that is ridiculous, make the main game included or don’t make the game.

It’s not a campaign if it’s locked behind a paywall; it’s a grift with a chance to sample it first.

developer_response.body:
[Update Review] We have removed the paywall.

Hello Nyt3cor3,

Thank you for sharing your story. It truly means a lot to us that Real VR Fishing could bring the joy of fishing back to you. That makes us even more sorry that the DLC requirement made you feel like you were hitting a wall.

We agree with you—the main game should never feel like a "30-minute sample" or a bait. We released an update in early February to fix this immediately.

Here is what has changed:

No Paywalls: You can now progress past the early Journey missions without buying any DLC.

Full Access: The maps required for the Journey are now free to access in this mode.

Uninterrupted Gameplay: You can enjoy the full campaign and become the "greatest fisherman" without being asked for more money.

We hope this allows you to get back on the water and enjoy fishing freely.
Thank you for your honest feedback to help us get this right.


Hi Nyt3cor3,

Thank you for taking the time to share your experience — and we’re genuinely sorry that this felt frustrating and misleading.

We want to be clear about one important point first:
The Journey was never intended to be locked behind DLC purchases.
Originally, all DLCs in Real VR Fishing were designed strictly as optional additional content, not as requirements to progress through the core experience.

However, with the recent Journey update, this separation was not implemented properly, especially for players coming in through Horizon+. As a result, it can feel like the base experience abruptly stops and asks for payment in order to continue — and we fully understand why that comes across as unfair.

That was not our intention.

We’re actively preparing an update that will:

Allow players to progress and complete the Journey without purchasing DLC

Treat DLC areas as optional experiences, including opportunities to preview that content before deciding whether to buy

We agree with you that a campaign or progression path should not feel like a paywall, and that Horizon+ players in particular should never feel like they’re being offered only a “sample” of the game.

Thank you for calling this out directly. Feedback like this is helping us correct the structure and better align the experience with what players reasonably expect from the base game.

developer_response.body:
Hi MysticBlueMopar1966,

Thanks for sharing your feedback — we understand how frustrating that experience can be.

Just to clarify one important point:
Rod & Reel with the same performance are also available through in-game currency, and IAP items are purely cosmetic. They do not provide any gameplay advantage or increased catch power.

Regarding the sharks, they are designed to be challenging encounters — especially during events — so even with strong gear, factors like timing and tension control still play a big role. That said, we recognize that the experience can feel frustrating, and we’re continuing to review balance based on player feedback.

We also appreciate your point about difficulty settings. Event sharks are currently tuned to provide a consistent challenge across difficulties, but we understand this may not always feel intuitive and are taking this feedback seriously.

If you’re able to share a short gameplay clip of the issue (especially when the line breaks), it would help us take a closer look: support@dugxr.com

Thanks again for taking the time to let us know — it really helps us improve the game.

Tight lines 🎣

reviews.review_description: This is a genuinely good arcade style fishing game, that being said the base game is basically a demo that is $20. Stop taking advantage of people who like and want to buy your finished product.

developer_response.body:
Hi 1_True_Horse,

Thanks so much for sharing your thoughts — we really hear you on the content and pricing side, and we appreciate you taking the time to write in.

A bit of context that might help: Real VR Fishing launched back in 2019 as a complete experience, and we supported it that way for quite a while before any DLCs came along. The DLCs you see today weren’t dropped all at once — they’ve been rolled out gradually since 2021, mostly in response to players asking for new spots and more variety. Over the years, that’s naturally added up to a longer list of optional expansions.

A few things worth mentioning:

- All gameplay updates and events are free — biweekly patches and monthly events included.
- Any microtransactions in-game are purely cosmetic — nothing gives a gameplay advantage.
- The base game itself includes 40+ maps and 100+ fish species, so there’s plenty to enjoy even without picking up any DLCs.

If you ever feel like exploring the game at your own pace, our Facebook community is a great place to hang out. A lot of our players take their time, slowly expanding their experience bit by bit — and it’s honestly one of the most fun ways to enjoy Real VR Fishing long-term.

Thanks again for the feedback — it really does help us. And if anything else is on your mind, don’t hesitate to reach out anytime.
Tight lines! 🎣

reviews.review_description: This game was kinda expensive but when I got it the fun was to the moon! I’m a beginner and I have some how got 12 sharks. Beginners luck am I right?

★★★★★ Surprisingly one of my favourite VR games

I’m not a fishing enthusiast at all, either in real life or in games, but this has completely converted me.

The scenery and water are so relaxing, and the fish graphics are honestly top notch. It’s a great game to just chill with, especially with YouTube music playing in the background.

I’ll be honest, when I first read some of the reviews talking about money-making DLC and calling it a con, it did put me off and made me wary of buying the game. I’ve had other games like that before, where they keep charging for extras but barely update the game and the devs never reply to anyone. So instead of buying it outright, I got it through Horizon+.

But after playing it, I can honestly say those reviews gave the wrong impression. This is not a dead game. The devs clearly care, they read and reply to review comments, and they keep adding updates and content. There is already loads of free content, so you definitely do not need to buy DLC to enjoy it. That said, the DLC is worth it, especially when some of it is only around £2.

I’ve only been playing for 3 days. I started on my own, then found out a lot of my friends already had it but hadn’t played for years. Now they’ve come back because the Megalodon, shark content and newer rods are all new to them. That says a lot about how much has been added to the game. It’s also really nice to play with friends.

What’s funny is we all love it so much now that we’ve started buying the DLCs anyway, even though we haven’t even finished all the free content yet.

So yes, I’m fully converted now. I love this game and so do my friends.

A few things we’d really love to see in future, if possible: being able to move around the beach, pier or fishing areas a bit more while fishing, wall-mounted or stuffed fish trophies, more friendly challenge modes when playing with friends like biggest fish in 10 minutes, most fish in 20 minutes, or catching a specific fish, a bigger aquarium or more than just two, and maybe even some fun themed locations in future like dinosaur, space or galactic fishing areas.

Overall though, brilliant game and one of the most relaxing VR experiences I’ve played.

reviews.review_description: This game used to be a great fishing experience, where you’d catch fish from scenic places. Now, it’s reduced to AI shovelware slop built upon predatory microtransactions. This game truly had potential to be an interesting experience, and is now built off cash an ai. Do better.

developer_response.body:
Hi Gourami2010,

Thank you for taking the time to share your feedback — we truly appreciate it, even when it’s critical.

First, regarding equipment: while it is true that some rods are available in the Premium Shop, they are not stronger than the best in-game gear. Players can obtain equipment with equivalent performance using in-game currency, and we are committed to maintaining a non pay-to-win experience.

We also understand your disappointment about the Amazon DLC. We sincerely apologize for not being able to deliver it so far. One of the main challenges has been the lack of sufficient high-quality reference assets from that region to meet our visual standards. That said, we’ve recently been shifting toward a more theme-based DLC approach, and we will revisit the Amazon concept internally to see how we can bring it to players in the future.

Regarding AI usage: while we may use AI during early concept exploration, all 3D assets in the game are created by our team. However, your point is valid — especially for areas like 2D banners — and we will make an effort to reduce reliance on AI-generated elements moving forward.

We’re sorry to hear that the recent direction hasn’t met your expectations. Our goal is still to deliver a relaxing and high-quality fishing experience, and feedback like yours plays an important role in helping us improve.

Thank you again for your honest input.

reviews.review_title: No Real VR Fishing Here, not anymore
reviews.review_description:
I don't understand why this app changed so much over the last several months for the worst. I loved fishing and as someone who's disabled and has a history of PTSD, I had tried this with a therapist and loved it. It was enjoyable, calming, and allowed me to visit areas I've been to and others I'll likely never visit in my life.

During the holidays, I purchased a Quest 3 and a number of add-ons to this, only to be greeted with all sorts of weird events going on. Christmas and holiday stuff that didn't really fit the theme and was a little triggering (I suffered my life changing event during the holidays that led to my issues, so that's a me thing. But it's also why I wanted a safe place to be free from such things if I felt too much stress).

Then they added sharks. Where the heck does anyone fish for sharks in a 'Real' VR Fishing game?  😭 And with the events comes FOMO. It's something I'm very carefully avoiding in my life, and yet it is added to the one thing I was intending to use to improve my quality of life. I remember reading a few months back a review where a dev said they were going to revert things back. It's been 3+ months and it's still a mess. So until that happens, I can't really recommend this, at least for anyone looking for an honestly clean 'real' fishing experience.

This app went from being one of the few things to providing me peace and relaxation to one of the most anxiety driving things out there. I'm at a point that I just don't launch this due to how stressful simply thinking about it is. I wish I could refund it but after getting the bundles and DLC, I'm just stuck.

Devs, please have a way to disable all the fluff and other junk many of us don't want and return the game to the clean and enjoyable experience it once was. Real VR Fishing should be exactly that. If you wanted to add all this other junk, it should be a spin-off game geared for a different user base.

developer_response.body:
Hi IonFONE,

Thank you for taking the time to share this. We truly appreciate how openly you described your experience, and we want to acknowledge the concerns you raised.

Real VR Fishing has always meant different things to different players. For some, it’s about variety and events; for others, it’s about calm, realism, and having a quiet space to relax. Your feedback strongly represents the latter group, and we see it as an important perspective — not something to dismiss or override.

We’ve received similar feedback from players who feel that recent seasonal themes, special events, and certain additions don’t align with the peaceful, grounded experience they originally connected with. Rather than viewing this as a matter of right or wrong, we see it as a difference in how players want to experience the game.

Because of that, our focus is shifting toward choice.

Instead of assuming one single direction works for everyone, we’re exploring ways to better separate core, clean gameplay from optional content, and to give players more control over what they see and engage with. This includes discussions around allowing players to opt out of events or themed elements if they prefer a more minimal, realistic experience.

We can’t promise specific features or timelines today, but we want to be clear that feedback like yours is actively shaping how we think about the future of Real VR Fishing — especially around accessibility, comfort, and mental well-being.

Thank you again for sharing your perspective. It’s being heard, and it matters.

reviews.review_title: Credits needed to fish the Derby, WTH?
reviews.review_description: Don't waste your time OR your money - I was enjoying Real VR Fishing, played a couple Derby's and spent REAL MONEY on extra's. Yesterday during the current Trout Derby - fishing to catch a bigger target fish with little success - when two kids joined my room. They were very forthcoming with advice on how to get a bigger target fish, all you have to do is "Reset your Fish" imagine my disappointment. Yup - don't like the fish you have let it go (hold right stick), it respawns bigger OR you can switch from Expert to Normal and use the glasses to target the fish at the surface, catch one and then go back to expert and get a whole pond of new bigger fish. Then today 12/22 when I log in it is now going to cost me "Credits" to continue the Championship?!? NOPE! Parents all I hear fishing right now are your kids - check your credit cards! I am certainly not going to support shady dealings like this!

developer_response.body: Thanks for your feedback — we understand why that was frustrating. And just to clarify a very big point, Credits needed for continued participation in the Championship are NOT real money. Credits are in-game currency — collected during play and are NOT paid for in the real world. Related: As for “reset fish” that you raised, those are not features we design as a competitive item but are a behavior some players have discovered. We acknowledge that this can compromise an overall fair play environment in competitive events and are currently reviewing best practices to enhance and balance the Championship experience. We appreciate your input and appreciate you taking the time to raise these concerns. We want the game to remain fair, transparent, and pleasurable for players. Thank you again for playing.

reviews.review_description:
I bought this game after playing it for a little on Horizon+. The game it self is very good. Very realistic graphics on most maps. The fish models are really good, the fishing action is fun and addictive, and the sound design is really good. Very relaxing, fun, and sometimes very exciting game. 

My only problem with this game is the DLC pack I bought. I bought the East+West coast DLC pack and base game in a bundle. One of my biggest problems with the DLC’s is pretty personal, but also really annoying if you like saltwater fishing.

The Florida locations suck. There’s about 12 species of fish, and no matter what bait or lures I use, I can only seem to catch 3 of the species. Haven’t even seen a shark yet. This problem also extends up the coast line. It’s  really disappointing to me because Florida has a vast fishing culture and many, many species in reality . I bought the DLC pack because I was excited about the Florida fishing aspect. It’s not the only DLC map that feels empty. I would really appreciate the development team putting a little more effor into their DLCs, and especially for the east coast saltwater maps.

developer_response.body:
Hi MikeyyyyG,

Thank you very much for your thoughtful feedback and for sharing your detailed experience with the DLC. We truly appreciate the kind words about the visuals, fish models, sound design, and overall gameplay — it means a lot to our team.

Regarding your experience in the Florida locations, one thing to note is that the size and variety of fish you encounter can be influenced by your level. If your level is still relatively low, you may see fewer or smaller species. Progressing your level through the Release mechanic can help unlock larger fish and expand the variety you encounter over time.

That said, we completely understand your expectations around Florida’s rich saltwater fishing culture. Your feedback is very valuable, and we’ll also take it into consideration as we explore ways to further improve and expand existing DLC areas, including Florida and other East Coast saltwater maps.

Thank you again for your support and for helping us make Real VR Fishing even better.
